subject: ap-precalculus
title: Function Concepts: Composition, Inverses & End Behavior
stimulus: The graph of function $f$ is shown on its domain $-3.5 \leq x \leq 3.5$. The points $(-3, 1)$, $(0, 1)$, and $(3, 1)$ are on the graph of $f$. The function $g$ is given by $g(x) = 2.916 \cdot (0.7)^x$.

parts.prompt:
(i) The function $h$ is defined by $h(x) = (g \circ f)(x) = g(f(x))$. Find the value of $h(3)$ as a decimal approximation, or indicate that it is not defined.

(ii) Find all values of $x$ for which $f(x) = 1$, or indicate that there are no such values.

scoring_points.description: Correct decimal approximation for $h(3) = g(f(3)) = g(1) = 2.916 \cdot (0.7)^1 = 2.041$
wrong_examples: $h(3) = 2.04$ (decimal presentation error — first such error does not earn the point)
common_traps: Forgetting to evaluate $f(3) = 1$ first before applying $g$
Reporting fewer than 3 decimal places (decimal presentation error)

scoring_points.description: All three correct values $x = -3$, $x = 0$, and $x = 3$ with no incorrect values included
common_traps: Missing one of the three values
Including incorrect extra values which voids the point

parts.scoring_notes: Decimal approximations must be correct to three places after the decimal point by rounding or truncating. The first decimal presentation error in Question 1 does not earn the point. For part (ii), no supporting work is required. A response that does not earn either point in Part (A) is eligible for partial credit (scored 0–1) if it provides one criterion from the first column (correct value in (i) not as decimal, or with decimal presentation error) AND one criterion from the second column (only one or two correct values in (ii) with no incorrect values).

parts.prompt:
(i) Find all values of $x$, as decimal approximations, for which $g(x) = 2$, or indicate that there are no such values.

(ii) Determine the end behavior of $g$ as $x$ increases without bound. Express your answer using the mathematical notation of a limit.

scoring_points.description: Correct decimal approximation $x = 1.057$ with no incorrect values included; from $2.916(0.7)^x = 2$
common_traps: Including extra incorrect solutions alongside the correct one

scoring_points.description: Correct limit statement with all four required components: 'lim', '$x \to \infty$', the function $g$, and the value $0$
common_traps: Omitting one of the four required limit notation components
If an additional complete limit statement is included (e.g., $\lim_{x \to -\infty} g(x) = \infty$), its value must be correct

parts.scoring_notes: The first point is earned for a correct decimal approximation of 1.057 with no incorrect values. The second point requires a correct limit statement with four components: 'lim,' '$x \to \infty$,' the function $g$, and $0$. Partial credit (scored 1–0) available if one criterion from each partial credit column is met.

parts.prompt:
(i) Determine if $f$ has an inverse function.

(ii) Give a reason for your answer based on the definition of a function and the graph of $y = f(x)$.

scoring_points.description: $f$ does not have an inverse function on its domain $-3.5 \leq x \leq 3.5$

required_elements: $f$ does not have an inverse function
alternatives.correct_example: $f$ does not have an inverse function on its domain $-3.5 \leq x \leq 3.5$

common_traps: Incorrectly stating $f$ has an inverse without checking one-to-one property

scoring_points.description: Reason must include an implicit or explicit reference to the definition of a function AND cite specific function values showing non-uniqueness, e.g., $f(-3) = f(0) = f(3) = 1$

required_elements: definition of a function (each output from unique input)
specific function values such as $f(-3) = f(0) = f(3) = 1$
alternatives.correct_example: There are output values of $f$ that are not mapped from unique input values; for example, $f(-3) = f(0) = f(3) = 1$

wrong_examples: '$f$ is not one-to-one' without specific values
'$f$ fails the horizontal line test' without citing function values
'There are output values not mapped from unique input values' without specific examples
common_traps: Citing the horizontal line test or one-to-one language without providing specific function values — this is insufficient for the second point
The second point cannot be earned if there are any errors in part (c)(ii)

parts.scoring_notes: Both points may be earned in part (ii) alone if no incorrect response appears in part (i). A response such as '$f$ does not have an inverse because $f(-3) = f(0) = 1$' earns both points. Responses such as '$f$ is not one-to-one,' '$f$ fails the horizontal line test,' or 'there are output values not mapped from unique input values' without specific function values are not sufficient for the second point. The second point cannot be earned if there are any errors in part (c)(ii).
